get.description:
Liveness probe — always returns 200 if the process is running.

Use this for Kubernetes/Docker liveness checks.

Readiness probe — checks that database and Redis are reachable.

Returns 200 with status "ok" if all dependencies are healthy,
or 200 with status "degraded" if some checks fail.

Use this for Kubernetes readiness probes or load balancer health checks.

Detailed system status for monitoring dashboards.

Includes database latency, Redis stats, WebSocket connections,
and market cache information.

List markets with live prices from cache.

Markets are sourced from the Redis market-list cache filled by the
background poller.  Each market is enriched with its latest cached
price data.

schema.title: Response List Markets V1 Markets Get

get.description:
Fetch a single market's detail with live price.

If ``include_book`` is true, the response also includes
a ``book`` key with the CLOB order-book snapshot (async, best-effort).

Fetch the CLOB order book for a market outcome.

Returns bids and asks with configurable depth.
Requires a valid token_id in the cache (from the price enrichment data).

post.description:
Fetch cached prices for multiple markets in one request.

Uses a Redis pipeline for efficient batch lookups.
Limited to 50 markets per request (adjustable per API tier).

schema.title: Response Batch Prices V1 Prices Batch Post

get.description:
Fetch OHLCV candles from the Polymarket CLOB /prices-history endpoint.

The CLOB returns price history keyed by token_id, not condition_id.
This endpoint resolves the token_id from the cached price data.

schema.title: Response Get Candles V1 Markets  Condition Id  Candles Get

post.description:
Place a market or limit order.

**Market orders** are executed immediately against the CLOB midpoint
via the main trading engine (book walking, slippage protection).

**Limit orders** are queued as PendingOrder rows and filled by the
background matching engine when the price condition is met.

get.description:
List pending, filled, and cancelled limit orders.

Supports both offset and cursor-based pagination:
- **Offset**: Use ``limit`` + ``offset`` query params.
- **Cursor**: Use the ``next_cursor`` value from a previous response as
  the ``cursor`` query param for the next page.  Cursor pagination is
  preferred for bots that iterate through large result sets.

post.description:
Place multiple orders in one request.

Each order is processed independently — individual failures don't
block the rest.  The batch size is limited by your API tier.

delete.description:
Cancel a pending limit order.

Refunds reserved funds (BUY) or restores reserved shares (SELL).
Only PENDING orders owned by the authenticated user can be cancelled.

post.description:
CLOB-compatible order endpoint.

Accepts orders using the same schema as Polymarket's real CLOB API
(token_id + price + size), enabling one-URL-swap migration to live.

Under the hood, resolves token_id → (condition_id, outcome) from
the Redis price cache, then delegates to the main trading engine.

get.description: Get the user's current cash balance, unrealized PnL, and total value.
List the user's positions with live valuations from Redis cache.

parameters.name: status

schema.description: Filter by status: OPEN or CLOSED
schema.title: Status

parameters.description: Filter by status: OPEN or CLOSED

schema.title: Response Get Positions V1 Account Positions Get

get.description: Get a combined portfolio overview: balance, positions, and win rate.
Get the user's trade history (filled orders), ordered by most recent.

schema.title: Response Get Trade History V1 Account History Get

get.description: Get the equity curve from hourly portfolio snapshots.

schema.title: Response Get Equity Curve V1 Account Equity Get

get.description: List all API keys for the authenticated user (masked).

schema.title: Response List Keys V1 Keys Get

post.description:
Generate a new API key.

The raw key is returned ONCE in the response.  Only the SHA-256 hash
is stored in the database.

delete.description:
Revoke (delete) an API key.

The key is permanently deleted.  Any active sessions using this key
will be rejected on the next request.

get.description: List all available rate limit tiers.

schema.title: Response List Tiers V1 Keys Tiers Get

post.description:
Mint a short-lived JWT for authenticating WebSocket connections.

Usage:
    1. Call this endpoint with X-API-Key header.
    2. Connect to ``ws://.../v1/ws/prices?token=<jwt>``
    3. Token expires in 60 seconds — reconnect with fresh token.

get.description: Minimal health check — proves the event loop is responsive.

Public: List active markets with optional search.

Cache Strategy (Redis-first):
1. Try Redis market list cache first (instant, no DB hit)
2. Add live prices from Redis price cache
3. Only fall back to DB on cache miss or for search queries

This ensures fast page loads even with slow/unavailable DB.

Public: List event groups with their markets and prices.

Returns pre-aggregated event groups for efficient frontend loading.
Each group contains:
- event_group_id, event_title, event_image
- markets: list of markets with live prices (sorted by probability)
- total_volume, market_count, is_hot

This endpoint eliminates the need for separate per-group API calls
that caused the "Person P" placeholder issue in MarketsGrid.

Public: Get available market categories with counts.

Returns categories derived from cached events, with event counts
and metadata for each category. Used by frontend for category filtering.

Public: List events with their markets (event-first data structure).

This endpoint provides the Polymarket-style event->markets hierarchy,
preserving the grouping from the Gamma API. Each event contains:
- event_group_id: Unique ID for the event
- event_title: Display title (e.g., "2028 Democratic Presidential Nomination")
- event_image: URL for event thumbnail
- markets: List of markets within this event, each with live prices
- total_volume: Sum of all market volumes
- market_count: Number of markets in this event
- is_hot: Whether this event has significant trading activity

Query params:
- limit: Max events to return (default 50)
- offset: Pagination offset
- hot_only: Only return events with significant volume
- markets_per_event: Max markets per event (default 20, for bandwidth)
- category: Filter by detected category (Sports, Politics, Crypto, etc.)
- search: Text search in event titles and market questions (searches sports too)
- include_sports: Whether to include sports games in search results (default true)

Public: Fast search preview endpoint for navbar typeahead.

Returns lightweight event/market cards (title, image, probability, slug)
suitable for rendering in a search dropdown without pagination overhead.

Sources (in priority order):
1. search:preview:{q} Redis cache (30s TTL) — fastest
2. search:events:{q} Redis cache (60s TTL) — existing search cache
3. DB direct query (≤20 rows, 5s timeout) — fallback for cold cache

Args:
    q:     Search query string (min 2 chars required, max 50 used)
    limit: Max results to return (default 8, max 12)

Public: Get a single event with all its markets.

Returns the full event structure including all markets with live prices.
This is useful for event detail pages or when you need complete market data
for a specific event group.

Accepts either event_group_id or event slug as the path parameter.

Public: List mention-style markets (tweets, speeches, quotes, etc.)

This endpoint returns events that contain markets about what public figures
will say, tweet, or mention. Includes:
- Tweet count markets (e.g., "How many tweets will Elon post?")
- Speech/statement markets (e.g., "What will be said during the Grammys?")
- Earnings call markets (e.g., "What will Google say during earnings?")
- Press briefing markets (e.g., "What will the White House say?")

Query params:
- limit: Max events to return (default 50)
- offset: Pagination offset  
- search: Text search in event titles and market questions
- markets_per_event: Max markets per event (default 20)

Data is pre-filtered server-side during market sync (no client-side filtering needed).

Public: List weather/meteorology/natural disaster markets.

Surfaces events related to:
- Temperature predictions (NYC, London, Seoul highs)
- Hurricanes and tropical storms
- Earthquakes and seismic activity
- Wildfires
- Climate records (hottest year, month records)
- Snow/winter storms
- Flooding and rainfall

Query params:
- limit: Max events to return (default 50)
- offset: Pagination offset
- search: Text search in event titles
- category: Filter by subcategory (hurricanes, temperature, earthquakes, wildfires, winter, flooding, climate, severe)
- markets_per_event: Max markets per event (default 20)

Get all current live prices from connected feeds.

Priority: Redis (WebSocket-sourced) > in-memory (Binance direct)

Returns prices for:
- Crypto (BTC, ETH, SOL, etc.) - from Polymarket RTDS or Binance WebSocket
- Stock indices (SPX, NDX, DJI) - placeholder data (requires paid feed)

Get live price for a specific symbol.

Priority: Redis (WebSocket-sourced) > in-memory (Binance direct)

Supported symbols:
- Crypto: BTC, ETH, SOL, BNB, XRP, DOGE
- Indices: SPX, NDX, DJI (placeholder)

Get price history for charting.

Returns the last N price points for the given symbol.

Get historical crypto price at a specific timestamp.

PRIMARY: Chainlink timeline sorted set (populated by RTDS WebSocket).
This gives us the exact Chainlink price that Polymarket uses for
Up/Down market resolution — accurate to the second for the last 30 min.

FALLBACK: CryptoCompare API (hourly candles) for older timestamps.

This is used for Up/Down markets to show the "price to beat" - 
the crypto price at the start of the market interval.

parameters.name: timestamp

schema.description: ISO timestamp to get price at (e.g. 2025-04-01T00:09:00Z)
schema.title: Timestamp

parameters.description: ISO timestamp to get price at (e.g. 2025-04-01T00:09:00Z)

get.description:
SSE endpoint for real-time price updates.

Streams market prices (from Polymarket CLOB WebSocket) and
crypto asset prices (from Polymarket RTDS WebSocket) to clients.

Query params:
- condition_ids: Filter to specific markets (comma-separated)
- crypto: Filter to specific crypto symbols (comma-separated)

Event types:
- market_price: Polymarket market price update (buy/sell probabilities)
- crypto_price: Crypto asset price update (BTC, ETH, etc.)
- keepalive: Periodic keepalive message
Get the status of Polymarket WebSocket connections including RTDS health.
Debug endpoint for WebSocket leader election and connection state.

Get all active "Up or Down" interval markets from Polymarket.

These are short-term binary markets that resolve based on whether
a price goes up or down within a specific time interval.

Supported assets:
- BTC (Bitcoin)
- ETH (Ethereum)
- SOL (Solana)
- XRP (Ripple)
- SPX (S&P 500)
- NDX (Nasdaq 100)

Supported intervals:
- 5M (5 minutes) - Requires server configuration flag=true
- 15M (15 minutes) - Requires server configuration flag=true
- 1H (1 hour)
- 4H (4 hours)
- daily

Note: 5M and 15M intervals are disabled by default to reduce database load.
Set server configuration flag=true in environment to enable them.

parameters.name: asset

schema.description: Filter by asset: BTC, ETH, SOL, XRP, SPX, NDX
schema.title: Asset

parameters.description: Filter by asset: BTC, ETH, SOL, XRP, SPX, NDX

get.description:
Public: List live and upcoming sports games with grouped markets.

Each game contains moneyline, spread, and totals markets.
Live games are sorted first, then by volume.

Query params:
- limit: max games to return (default 50)
- offset: pagination offset
- league: filter by league (NFL, NBA, MLB, etc.)
- live_only: only return games currently in progress

Public: List sports futures markets (championships, MVP, etc.).

Each futures market contains multiple outcomes with their probabilities.
Sorted by total volume.

Query params:
- limit: max futures to return (default 20)
- offset: pagination offset
- league: filter by league (NFL, NBA, etc.)

Look up a market by its slug for short URL support.
Returns the market with its condition_id for redirect purposes.
Useful for /go/[slug] short URLs for easy sharing on X/Twitter.

Also handles condition_id inputs gracefully — the frontend sometimes
sends condition_ids to this endpoint (e.g., from generateMetadata parallel
fetches). If the input looks like a condition_id (starts with 0x), we
proxy to the condition_id lookup path instead of 404ing.

Cache-first: Checks Redis slug→condition_id mapping before DB.

Get a single market by condition_id with live price data.

ASYNC: Non-blocking Gamma API calls (no worker blocking on HTTP).

Protected: Get user balance and info.
Cached in Redis for 60s to reduce transatlantic latency.
Cache-Control: private (not cached by Cloudflare/edge CDN).

put.description:
Update user profile settings.

Avatar Configuration:
- Set avatar_seed to customize your Dicebear Notionists avatar
- Avatar URL is auto-generated: https://api.dicebear.com/7.x/notionists/svg?seed={avatar_seed}
- Preview different seeds at: https://www.dicebear.com/styles/notionists/

Username Rules:
- 3-50 characters, alphanumeric and underscores only
- Must be unique (case-insensitive)

Privacy Rules:
- Username REQUIRED for public profile visibility
- Username REQUIRED to appear on leaderboard
- Emails are NEVER exposed publicly

NOTE: This endpoint is backend-ready but UI is not yet deployed.

get.description:
PUBLIC leaderboard endpoint - PRIVACY SAFE.

IMPORTANT PRIVACY RULES:
- ONLY shows users who have EXPLICITLY opted-in (show_on_leaderboard=true)
- ONLY shows users who have set a username
- ONLY shows users with public profile visibility
- NEVER exposes email addresses
- Uses display_name or username only

Performance: Serves from Redis cache (refreshed every 15 min by background task).
Falls back to DB query on cache miss.

This endpoint is safe for GDPR compliance.

Preview a Dicebear Notionists avatar with a given seed.
Returns the avatar URL for preview purposes.

Try different seeds to find one you like, then update your profile.

post.description:
Emergency exit a stuck position at fair value.

This allows users to exit positions in markets that are:
- Unavailable (no price data)
- Stale (not synced recently)
- Resolved but settlement failed

Exit price is calculated as:
1. If market is resolved: $1.00 for winners, $0.00 for losers
2. If market has last known price: Use that price
3. Otherwise: Use the user's average entry price (break-even)

Users can only exit their own positions.

get.description: Get user's positions that might be stuck (no recent price data).

post.description:
Lightweight page view tracking endpoint.
No auth required - uses hashed visitor fingerprint for unique counting.
Designed for accurate, first-party analytics independent of ad blockers.
Now captures device brand, browser, and OS for enterprise analytics.

get.description:
Protected: Get comprehensive trading analytics and PolyScore.

Returns detailed statistics calculated from the immutable ledger:
- PolyScore (0-1000): A composite score measuring trading skill
- Trading statistics: win rate, profit factor, Sharpe-like metrics
- Category breakdowns: Performance by market type
- Time-based analysis: Best/worst periods, trading patterns
- Risk metrics: Max drawdown, volatility, position sizing

All data is derived from ledger_entries, orders, and positions tables
to ensure historical accuracy and auditability.

Protected: Get user's open positions with current market values.
Cached in Redis for 90s to reduce transatlantic latency and DB pressure.

post.description:
Reset user's account: set balance to $1000 and close all positions.
This is a destructive operation but useful for starting fresh.

IMPORTANT: Creates a backup before reset to allow recovery if needed.
Backups are stored in the account_reset_backups table.

get.description:
Admin: List account reset backups for potential recovery.
Can filter by user_id or list all recent backups.

post.description:
Admin: Trigger immediate settlement of all stuck Up/Down positions.

This finds open positions with outcome "Up" or "Down" on expired markets
and settles them:
- If Gamma API has resolution: winners get $1/share, losers get $0
- If no resolution after 1 hour: emergency exit at entry price (break-even)

post.summary: Admin Settle Stuck Markets Via Clob
post.description:
Admin: Settle stuck markets using CLOB API for resolution detection.

Finds open positions on markets that our DB shows as active but which
have actually been resolved on Polymarket. Uses the CLOB API
(GET /markets/{condition_id}) which reliably returns resolution status,
unlike the broken Gamma API single-market lookup.

For each stuck market:
1. Fetch from CLOB API to check if closed/resolved
2. If resolved: settle normally (winners get $1/share, losers get $0)
3. Also updates market DB record (active=False, closed=True, resolved_outcome)

Query params:
- dry_run: If true, report what would be settled without making changes
- limit: Max markets to check per call (default 50, to avoid timeout)

Admin: Find and settle orphaned positions on resolved markets.

Catches positions that were marked CLOSED by the sell code path without
proper exit tracking, or positions created after settlement already ran.

Query params:
- dry_run: If true (default), report what would be settled without making changes

Admin: Recover a user's account from a reset backup.
This restores their positions, orders, ledger entries, and balance.

get.description:
Protected: Get user's recently settled (resolved market) positions.

Returns closed positions from resolved markets with payout information,
including total payout, P&L, and original quantity.
Protected: Get user's trade history.

Get live order book for a token.
Priority: WebSocket cache (real-time) > REST cache (3s TTL) > CLOB API fetch.

Get price history for a token.
Cached for 5-60 minutes depending on interval.
Only caches non-empty responses.

Fetch relevant tweets for a market using 3-tier caching:
1. Redis (fast, 1 hour TTL)
2. Supabase (persistent, 24 hour TTL)
3. Apify API (fallback, rate-limited)

Fetch user comments for a market from Polymarket's Gamma API.

Strategy (optimized for minimal API calls):
1. If event_id is provided (from frontend), use it directly.
2. Otherwise, resolve event_id from Redis caches (no API call).
3. If we have an event_id, fetch event-level comments (most comments live here).
4. Fall back to fetching market metadata from Gamma to get event_id.
5. Results cached in Redis for 5 minutes.

For updown markets: the frontend passes the series event_id so all
intervals share one comment section — no per-interval fetching needed.

DISCLAIMER: Comments are user-generated content from polymarket.com.

Public endpoint to get current site status notification.
Returns null if no active status.

Comprehensive dashboard statistics for admin panel.
Includes user metrics, trading analytics, market stats, and retention data.

Enterprise-grade analytics: retention cohorts, funnels, geo, devices, referrers, growth KPIs.
Designed for data-driven growth decisions.

Export analytics as structured plain-text report for LLM / MCP consumption.

Returns a well-formatted text document with all analytics sections, 
optimized for large-language-model ingestion and analysis.

Query params:
  - sections: comma-separated list to include (default: all)

Returns: { report: str, generated_at: str, sections_included: list, token_estimate: int }

Return all waitlist signups for admin review.
Also returns counts per source (backtesting / api / general).
Get all feedback entries for admin review with filtering.

Simple endpoint to verify admin access.
Returns admin info if access is granted.

Get all users with search and pagination.
Search works on email and user ID.
Get detailed info about a specific user including all transactions.

Get all trades with optional filters.
Supports real-time polling for live feed.

Get trades since a timestamp for live feed polling.
Optimized for frequent polling.
Admin: Get all comments for moderation.

get.description: Admin: Get all user reports for review.

Submit user feedback, bug reports, or feature requests.
Accepts both authenticated and anonymous submissions.

Get a user's public profile by username.
Returns profile info, follower counts, and optionally trades/PnL if public.
Get a user's public trades (if show_trades is enabled).

get.description: Get current user's social links.

post.description: Add a reaction to a trade.

delete.description: Remove a reaction from a trade.

get.description: Get reactions for a trade.

post.description: Add a comment to a trade.

get.description: Get comments for a trade.

post.description: Report a user or comment for moderation.

get.description: Get list of users following me.
Get list of users I'm following.

post.description:
GDPR-compliant waitlist signup.

- Requires explicit consent (must be True in request body)
- Deduplicates on (lower(email), source) — same user can join both waitlists
- Captures consent_version='v1' and consented_at for legal audit trail
- Rate-limited at reverse-proxy level; IP stored for dedup only

delete.description:
Soft-delete (right-to-withdraw) — sets status='unsubscribed'.
We keep the record for legal audit; we never email unsubscribed entries.

post.description: Clear all event-related caches (events list, event groups, individual events, mentions).

Clear market cache. If condition_id is provided, clear only that market.
Otherwise, clear all market caches (including updown).

get.description:
Get all positions that might be stuck (market not synced in N hours).
Admin only.

post.description:
Force settle a market with a specific outcome.
Admin only. Use with caution.

Args:
    market_id: The condition_id of the market
    winning_outcome: The winning outcome (e.g., "Yes", "No")
    dry_run: If true, just calculate payouts without making changes

Emergency exit a single position at a specified price.
Admin only.

Args:
    position_id: The position ID to exit
    exit_price: Price per share (0-1)
    reason: Reason for exit ('admin_close', 'market_unavailable', etc.)

Immediately trigger the legacy market check and settlement process.
This fetches all markets with open positions from Gamma API and
settles any that have been resolved.

get.description: Get all markets currently in the settlement queue.
Admin endpoint to get current status with metadata.

get.description: Get list of muted users.
